1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}
233

3	import { BuildOptions } from './build'
4	
5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
46	    emitTypes: true,
47	  },
48	  {
49	    name: 'esm',
50	    format: 'esm',
51	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
52	    outdir: 'dist',
53	    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
54	    emitTypes: true,
55	  },
56	]

input.description: Explore DMMF types and generator output
input.prompt:
I need to understand the DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) types and how generators use them to produce output. Please explore:

1. Search for DMMF type definitions - look in `packages/generator-helper/` and `packages/dmmf/` for the DMMF document structure, especially:
   - Model definitions (name, fields, etc.)
   - Field definitions (name, type, kind, isRequired, isList, etc.)
   - How relations are represented
   - The full GeneratorOptions type that's passed to generators

2. Look at how `packages/client-generator-ts/` processes DMMF models to generate TypeScript code. Read the file generators, especially any that generate model types/interfaces.

3. Look at `packages/ts-builders/` to understand the TypeScript code generation utilities available (interfaces, types, properties, doc comments).

4. Check how the generator-helper `generatorHandler` function works - what callbacks it expects and how it communicates with the Prisma CLI.

5. Look at test patterns for generators - how are generator packages tested? Check `packages/client-generator-ts/src/__tests__/` or `packages/client-generator-js/src/__tests__/`.

Be thorough - read actual source code for DMMF types and the generator handler protocol.

1	import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'
2	
3	export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
4	  datamodel: Datamodel
5	  schema: Schema
6	  mappings: Mappings
7	}>
8	
9	export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
10	  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
11	  otherOperations: {
12	    read: string[]
13	    write: string[]
14	  }
15	}>
16	
17	export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
18	  read: string[]
19	  write: string[]
20	}>
21	
22	export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
23	  name: string
24	  values: EnumValue[]
25	  dbName?: string | null
26	  documentation?: string
27	}>
28	
29	export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
30	  name: string
31	  values: string[]
32	}>
33	
34	export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
35	  name: string
36	  dbName: string | null
37	}>
38	
39	export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
40	  models: Model[]
41	  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
42	  types: Model[]
43	  indexes: Index[]
44	}>
45	
46	export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
47	  name: string
48	  fields: string[]
49	}>
50	export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
51	  name: string | null
52	  fields: string[]
53	}>
54	export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
55	  name: string
56	  dbName: string | null
57	  schema: string | null
58	  fields: Field[]
59	  uniqueFields: string[][]
60	  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
61	  documentation?: string
62	  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
63	  isGenerated?: boolean
64	}>
65	
66	export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
67	
68	export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
69	export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'
70	
71	export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
72	  kind: FieldKind
73	  name: string
74	  isRequired: boolean
75	  isList: boolean
76	  isUnique: boolean
77	  isId: boolean
78	  isReadOnly: boolean
79	  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
80	  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'

13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}

30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}

6	export function createModelFile(context: GenerateContext, modelName: string): string {
7	  const jsDocHeader = `/*
8	 * This file exports the \`${modelName}\` model and its related types.
9	 *
10	 * 🟢 You can import this file directly.
11	 */
12	`
13	
14	  const imports = [
15	    ts.moduleImport(context.runtimeImport).asNamespace('runtime').typeOnly(),
16	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName(`../enums`)).asNamespace('$Enums').typeOnly(),
17	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName(`../internal/prismaNamespace`)).asNamespace('Prisma').typeOnly(),
18	  ]
19	  const importsString = imports.map((i) => ts.stringify(i)).join('\n')
20	
21	  const model = context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap[modelName]
22	
23	  return jsDocHeader + importsString + '\n' + new Model(model, context).toTS()
24	}

4	
5	export abstract class TypeBuilder implements BasicBuilder {
6	  // TODO(@SevInf): this should be replaced with precedence system that would
7	  // automatically add parenthesis where they are needed
8	  needsParenthesisWhenIndexed = false
9	  needsParenthesisInKeyof = false
10	  needsParenthesisInUnion = false
11	  needsParenthesisInIntersection = false
12	
13	  abstract write(writer: Writer): void
14	
15	  subKey(key: string): KeyType {
16	    // TODO: since we translating ESM to CommonJS during bundling, we
17	    // can't handle circular dependencies the same way ESM does. We have to delay KeyType import
18	    // to resolve it. Once we stop doing this, we can move this import to top of the file.
19	    const { KeyType } = require('./KeyType')
20	    return new KeyType(this, key)
21	  }
22	
23	  writeIndexed(writer: Writer) {
24	    if (this.needsParenthesisWhenIndexed) {
25	      writer.write('(')
26	    }
27	
28	    writer.write(this)
29	
30	    if (this.needsParenthesisWhenIndexed) {
31	      writer.write(')')
32	    }
33	  }
34	}

Excellent! I now have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive guide document:

## Summary: Prisma Monorepo Build & Configuration System

I've thoroughly explored the build configuration system in the Prisma monorepo. Here's a comprehensive guide for creating new packages:

### **1. Build System Overview**

**Key Files:**
- `/helpers/compile/build.ts` - Main build orchestrator using esbuild
- `/helpers/compile/configs.ts` - Pre-configured build configurations
- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` - Root tsconfig with path mappings for bundled builds

**Two Build Strategies:**

1. **`adapterConfig`** (Bundled): Creates single `.js` and `.mjs` files
   - Used by: adapter packages, client generators
   - Creates 2 builds per package: CommonJS and ESM
   - Output: `dist/index.js`, `dist/index.mjs`

2. **`unbundledConfig`** (Unbundled): Multiple files per module
   - Used by: generic packages like `generator`, `client-common`, `dmmf`
   - Creates separate builds for each source file
   - Output: `dist/**/*.js`, `dist/**/*.mjs`

### **2. Build Configuration (`helpers/compile/configs.ts`)**

```typescript
// adapterConfig - for bundled single-entry packages
export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([
  { entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }
])
// Produces: dist/index.js (CJS), dist/index.mjs (ESM)

// unbundledConfig - for multi-file packages
export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
  {
    name: 'cjs',
    format: 'cjs',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
  {
    name: 'esm',
    format: 'esm',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
]
```

### **3. File Structure for New Packages**

Every package needs these files:

#### **`package.json`** (Template - Unbundled)
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/package-name",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/package-name"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/other-package": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

**For bundled packages (adapters):** Use the same structure, but the `exports` `types` field points to `.d.ts` (not `.d.mts`).

#### **`tsconfig.json`**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

#### **`tsconfig.build.json`**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

#### **`helpers/build.ts`** (Unbundled)
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { unbundledConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(unbundledConfig)
```

**Or for bundled packages:**
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

#### **Directory Structure**
```
packages/my-package/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          (main entry point)
│   └── [other files].ts
├── tests/                (optional, for unit tests)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts          (imports build config and runs it)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
└── README.md
```

### **4. Build Behavior Details**

**esbuild Configuration** (`helpers/compile/build.ts` defaults):
- Platform: `node`
- Target: `ES2022`
- Default entry points: `./src/**/*.{j,t}s` (excluding `src/__tests__/**/*`)
- Default output: `dist/`
- Module format: CJS by default, ESM variants use `.mjs` extension
- Plugins: `resolvePathsPlugin`, `fixImportsPlugin`, `tscPlugin`, `onErrorPlugin`
- TypeScript config: Uses `tsconfig.build.json` in each package

**External Dependencies:**
- Peer dependencies and regular dependencies are marked as external (unless `bundle: true`)
- Dev dependencies are bundled when `bundle: true`
- Can override with `IGNORE_EXTERNALS` env var

### **5. Path Mappings** (`tsconfig.build.bundle.json`)

The root path mappings enable imports like `@prisma/client-common` instead of relative paths:

```json
{
  "@prisma/package-name": ["./packages/package-name/src"]
}
```

Each new package should have an entry added to `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`.

### **6. PNPM Workspace** (`pnpm-workspace.yaml`)

```yaml
packages:
  - 'packages/*'
```

All packages in `packages/` are automatically included in the workspace. Packages use `workspace:*` in dependencies to reference each other.

### **7. Build Commands**

For any package:
```bash
pnpm run build          # Single build
pnpm run dev           # Watch mode (DEV=true)
pnpm run test          # Run tests
```

### **Complete Checklist for New Package**

1. Create directory: `packages/my-package/`
2. Create `package.json` with name, exports, and scripts
3. Create `tsconfig.json` (extends root)
4. Create `tsconfig.build.json` (extends `tsconfig.build.regular.json`)
5. Create `helpers/build.ts` (imports build config)
6. Create `src/index.ts` and other source files
7. Add path mapping to `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` if using path aliases
8. Add workspace dependency entries to other packages' `package.json` if needed
9. Run `pnpm install` to update lock file
10. Test with `pnpm -F my-package build`

---

**Absolute File Paths for Reference:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775502645174-4ottt0/helpers/compile/build.ts`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775502645174-4ottt0/helpers/compile/configs.ts`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775502645174-4ottt0/tsconfig.build.bundle.json`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775502645174-4ottt0/tsconfig.build.regular.json`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775502645174-4ottt0/pnpm-workspace.yaml`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775502645174-4ottt0/packages/generator/` (unbundled example)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775502645174-4ottt0/packages/adapter-pg/` (bundled example)

    format: 'cjs',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
  {
    name: 'esm',
    format: 'esm',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
]
```

### **3. File Structure for New Packages**

Every package needs these files:

#### **`package.json`** (Template - Unbundled)
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/package-name",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/package-name"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/other-package": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

**For bundled packages (adapters):** Use the same structure, but the `exports` `types` field points to `.d.ts` (not `.d.mts`).

#### **`tsconfig.json`**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

#### **`tsconfig.build.json`**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

#### **`helpers/build.ts`** (Unbundled)
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { unbundledConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(unbundledConfig)
```

**Or for bundled packages:**
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

#### **Directory Structure**
```
packages/my-package/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          (main entry point)
│   └── [other files].ts
├── tests/                (optional, for unit tests)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts          (imports build config and runs it)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
└── README.md
```

### **4. Build Behavior Details**

**esbuild Configuration** (`helpers/compile/build.ts` defaults):
- Platform: `node`
- Target: `ES2022`
- Default entry points: `./src/**/*.{j,t}s` (excluding `src/__tests__/**/*`)
- Default output: `dist/`
- Module format: CJS by default, ESM variants use `.mjs` extension
- Plugins: `resolvePathsPlugin`, `fixImportsPlugin`, `tscPlugin`, `onErrorPlugin`
- TypeScript config: Uses `tsconfig.build.json` in each package

**External Dependencies:**
- Peer dependencies and regular dependencies are marked as external (unless `bundle: true`)
- Dev dependencies are bundled when `bundle: true`
- Can override with `IGNORE_EXTERNALS` env var

### **5. Path Mappings** (`tsconfig.build.bundle.json`)

The root path mappings enable imports like `@prisma/client-common` instead of relative paths:

```json
{
  "@prisma/package-name": ["./packages/package-name/src"]
}
```

Each new package should have an entry added to `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`.

### **6. PNPM Workspace** (`pnpm-workspace.yaml`)

```yaml
packages:
  - 'packages/*'
```

All packages in `packages/` are automatically included in the workspace. Packages use `workspace:*` in dependencies to reference each other.

### **7. Build Commands**

For any package:
```bash
pnpm run build          # Single build
pnpm run dev           # Watch mode (DEV=true)
pnpm run test          # Run tests
```

### **Complete Checklist for New Package**

1. Create directory: `packages/my-package/`
2. Create `package.json` with name, exports, and scripts
3. Create `tsconfig.json` (extends root)
4. Create `tsconfig.build.json` (extends `tsconfig.build.regular.json`)
5. Create `helpers/build.ts` (imports build config)
6. Create `src/index.ts` and other source files
7. Add path mapping to `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` if using path aliases
8. Add workspace dependency entries to other packages' `package.json` if needed
9. Run `pnpm install` to update lock file
10. Test with `pnpm -F my-package build`

---

94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value

103	}

236	function writeFileMap(outputDir: string, fileMap: FileMap) {

245	        await fs.writeFile(absolutePath, content)
246	      } else {
247	        // subdirectory
248	        await fs.mkdir(absolutePath)
249	        await writeFileMap(absolutePath, content)
250	      }
251	    }),
252	  )
253	}

255	function validateDmmfAgainstDenylists(prismaClientDmmf: DMMF.Document): Error[] | null {
256	  const errorArray = [] as Error[]
257	
258	  const denylists = {
259	    // A copy of this list is also in prisma-engines. Any edit should be done in both places.
260	    // https://github.com/prisma/prisma-engines/blob/main/psl/parser-database/src/names/reserved_model_names.rs
261	    models: [
262	      // Reserved Prisma keywords
263	      'PrismaClient',
264	      'Prisma',
265	      // JavaScript keywords
266	      'async',
267	      'await',
268	      'break',
269	      'case',
270	      'catch',
271	      'class',
272	      'const',
273	      'continue',
274	      'debugger',
275	      'default',
276	      'delete',
277	      'do',
278	      'else',
279	      'enum',
280	      'export',
281	      'extends',
282	      'false',
283	      'finally',
284	      'for',
285	      'function',
286	      'if',
287	      'implements',
288	      'import',
289	      'in',
290	      'instanceof',
291	      'interface',
292	      'let',
293	      'new',
294	      'null',
295	      'package',
296	      'private',
297	      'protected',
298	      'public',
299	      'return',
300	      'super',
301	      'switch',
302	      'this',
303	      'throw',
304	      'true',
305	      'try',
306	      'using',
307	      'typeof',
308	      'var',
309	      'void',
310	      'while',
311	      'with',
312	      'yield',
313	    ],
314	    fields: ['AND', 'OR', 'NOT'],
315	    dynamic: [],
316	  }
317	
318	  if (prismaClientDmmf.datamodel.enums) {
319	    for (const it of prismaClientDmmf.datamodel.enums) {
320	      if (denylists.models.includes(it.name) || denylists.fields.includes(it.name)) {
321	        errorArray.push(Error(`"enum ${it.name}"`))
322	      }
323	    }
324	  }
325	
326	  if (prismaClientDmmf.datamodel.models) {
327	    for (const it of prismaClientDmmf.datamodel.models) {
328	      if (denylists.models.includes(it.name) || denylists.fields.includes(it.name)) {
329	        errorArray.push(Error(`"model ${it.name}"`))
330	      }
331	    }
332	  }
333	
334	  return errorArray.length > 0 ? errorArray : null
335	}
336	
337	/**
338	 * Get all the directories involved in the generation process.
339	 */
340	async function getGenerationDirs({ runtimeBase, outputDir }: GenerateClientOptions) {
341	  const normalizedOutputDir = path.normalize(outputDir)
342	  const normalizedRuntimeBase = pathToPosix(runtimeBase)
343	
344	  const userPackageRoot = await packageUp({ cwd: path.dirname(normalizedOutputDir) })
345	  const userProjectRoot = userPackageRoot ? path.dirname(userPackageRoot) : process.cwd()
346	
347	  return {
348	    runtimeBase: normalizedRuntimeBase,
349	    outputDir: normalizedOutputDir,
350	    projectRoot: userProjectRoot,
351	  }
352	}
353	
354	function getRuntimeNameForTarget(target: RuntimeTargetInternal): RuntimeName {
355	  switch (target) {
356	    case 'nodejs':
357	    case 'deno':
358	      return 'client'
359	
360	    case 'workerd':
361	    case 'vercel-edge':
362	      return 'wasm-compiler-edge'
363	
364	    default:
365	      assertNever(target, 'Unknown runtime target')
366	  }
367	}
368	
369	async function deleteOutputDir(outputDir: string) {
370	  try {
371	    const files = await fs.readdir(outputDir)
372	
373	    if (files.length === 0) {
374	      return
375	    }
376	
377	    if (
378	      !files.includes('client.ts') &&
379	      !files.includes('client.mts') &&
380	      !files.includes('client.cts') &&
381	      !files.includes('client.d.ts') // for legacy js client
382	    ) {
383	      // Make sure users don't accidentally wipe their source code or home directory.
384	      throw new Error(
385	        `${outputDir} exists and is not empty but doesn't look like a generated Prisma Client. ` +
386	          'Please check your output path and remove the existing directory if you indeed want to generate the Prisma Client in that location.',
387	      )
388	    }
389	
390	    await Promise.allSettled(
391	      (
392	        await glob(
393	          [
394	            `${outputDir}/**/*.{js,ts,mts,cts,d.ts}`,
395	            `${outputDir}/**/*.wasm`,
396	            `${outputDir}/*.node`,
397	            `${outputDir}/{query,schema}-engine-*`,
398	            `${outputDir}/package.json`,
399	            `${outputDir}/**/*.prisma`,
400	          ],
401	          {
402	            followSymbolicLinks: false,
403	          },
404	        )
405	      ).map((file) => fs.unlink(file)),
406	    )
407	  } catch (error) {
408	    if ((error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code !== 'ENOENT') {
409	      throw error
410	    }
411	  }
412	}

40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules

111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {

27	   */

29	    if (typeof value === 'string') {

31	    } else {

37	  /**

40	   *
41	   * @param separator
42	   * @param values
43	   * @param writeItem allow to customize how individual item is written
44	   * @returns
45	   */
46	  writeJoined<T extends string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>>(
47	    separator: string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>,
48	    values: T[],
49	    writeItem: (item: T, writer: this) => void = (item, w) => w.write(item),
50	  ): this {
51	    const last = values.length - 1
52	    for (let i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {

40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime

59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'

61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)

76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,

18	export class GenerateContext implements GenerateContextOptions {
19	  dmmf: DMMFHelper

28	  constructor({

1	import type { O } from 'ts-toolbelt'
2	
3	import { DMMFHelper } from '../dmmf'
4	import { generatedFileNameMapper, importFileNameMapper } from '../file-extensions'
5	import type { FileMap } from '../generateClient'
6	import { GenerateClientOptions } from '../generateClient'
7	import { GenericArgsInfo } from '../GenericsArgsInfo'
8	import { createBrowserFile } from './file-generators/BrowserFile'
9	import { createClassFile } from './file-generators/ClassFile'
10	import { createClientFile } from './file-generators/ClientFile'
11	import { createCommonInputTypeFiles } from './file-generators/CommonInputTypesFile'
12	import { createEnumsFile } from './file-generators/EnumsFile'
13	import { createModelFile } from './file-generators/ModelFile'
14	import { createModelsFile } from './file-generators/ModelsFile'
15	import { createPrismaNamespaceBrowserFile } from './file-generators/PrismaNamespaceBrowserFile'
16	import { createPrismaNamespaceFile } from './file-generators/PrismaNamespaceFile'
17	import { GenerateContext } from './GenerateContext'
18	
19	export type RuntimeName = 'wasm-compiler-edge' | 'client' | (string & {})
20	
21	export type TSClientOptions = O.Required<GenerateClientOptions, 'runtimeBase'> & {
22	  /** The name of the runtime bundle to use */
23	  runtimeName: RuntimeName
24	  /** When we are generating an edge-compatible client */
25	  edge: boolean
26	}
27	
28	export class TSClient {
29	  protected readonly dmmf: DMMFHelper
30	  protected readonly genericsInfo: GenericArgsInfo
31	
32	  constructor(protected readonly options: TSClientOptions) {
33	    this.dmmf = new DMMFHelper(options.dmmf)
34	    this.genericsInfo = new GenericArgsInfo(this.dmmf)
35	  }
36	
37	  generateClientFiles(): FileMap {
38	    const context = new GenerateContext({
39	      dmmf: this.dmmf,
40	      genericArgsInfo: this.genericsInfo,
41	      runtimeBase: this.options.runtimeBase,
42	      runtimeImport: `${this.options.runtimeBase}/${this.options.runtimeName}`,
43	      outputFileName: generatedFileNameMapper(this.options.generatedFileExtension),
44	      importFileName: importFileNameMapper(this.options.importFileExtension),
45	      generator: this.options.generator,
46	      provider: this.options.activeProvider,
47	    })
48	
49	    const modelNames = Object.values(context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap)
50	      .filter((model) => context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[model.name])
51	      .map((model) => model.name)
52	
53	    const modelsFileMap: FileMap = modelNames.reduce((acc, modelName) => {
54	      acc[context.outputFileName(modelName)] = createModelFile(context, modelName)
55	      return acc
56	    }, {})
57	
58	    return {
59	      [context.outputFileName('client')]: createClientFile(context, this.options),
60	      [context.outputFileName('browser')]: createBrowserFile(context, this.options),
61	      [context.outputFileName('enums')]: createEnumsFile(context),
62	      [context.outputFileName('commonInputTypes')]: createCommonInputTypeFiles(context),
63	      [context.outputFileName('models')]: createModelsFile(context, modelNames),
64	      models: modelsFileMap,
65	      internal: {
66	        [context.outputFileName('prismaNamespace')]: createPrismaNamespaceFile(context, this.options),
67	        [context.outputFileName('prismaNamespaceBrowser')]: createPrismaNamespaceBrowserFile(context),
68	        [context.outputFileName('class')]: createClassFile(context, this.options),
69	      },
70	    }
71	  }
72	}

1	import { GenerateContext } from '../GenerateContext'
2	
3	const jsDocHeader = `/*
4	 * This is a barrel export file for all models and their related types.
5	 *
6	 * 🟢 You can import this file directly.
7	 */
8	`
9	
10	export function createModelsFile(context: GenerateContext, modelsNames: string[]): string {
11	  const exports = modelsNames.map((m) => `export type * from './models/${context.importFileName(m)}'`)
12	  exports.push(`export type * from './${context.importFileName('commonInputTypes')}'`)
13	  return jsDocHeader + exports.join('\n')
14	}

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary document.

## DMMF Types and Generator Architecture - Comprehensive Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the Prisma codebase, here's a complete understanding of the DMMF system and how generators use it:

### 1. DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) Type Definitions

**Location:** `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

The DMMF is a complete representation of your data model in an intermediate format. Key structures:

#### Document (Root)
```
Document = {
  datamodel: Datamodel        // Models, enums, types, indexes
  schema: Schema              // GraphQL-like schema for API
  mappings: Mappings          // Maps models to their CRUD operations
}
```

#### Datamodel
```
Datamodel = {
  models: Model[]             // Database models
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]      // Enum definitions
  types: Model[]              // Composite types (same structure as models)
  indexes: Index[]            // Database indexes
}
```

#### Model
- **name**: string - Model name
- **dbName**: string | null - Database table name
- **schema**: string | null - Database schema
- **fields**: Field[] - All fields of the model
- **uniqueFields**: string[][] - Unique constraints
- **uniqueIndexes**: uniqueIndex[] - Unique indexes
- **primaryKey**: PrimaryKey | null - Primary key definition
- **documentation**: string - JSDoc comments
- **isGenerated**: boolean - If auto-generated

#### Field (Core Data Unit)
```typescript
Field = {
  kind: 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  type: string                    // e.g., 'Int', 'String', 'DateTime', '$ModelName'
  isRequired: boolean             // NOT NULL constraint
  isList: boolean                 // Is array/list
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean                   // Primary key
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean           // @updatedAt timestamp
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] // e.g., ['VarChar', ['191']]
  dbName?: string | null          // Column name in DB
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  
  // For relations:
  relationFromFields?: string[]   // Foreign key fields
  relationToFields?: string[]     // Referenced fields
  relationOnDelete?: string       // CASCADE, SET NULL, etc.
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string           // Relation name
  documentation?: string
}
```

#### Schema (GraphQL-like Type System)
```
Schema = {
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    model?: InputType[]     // User model input types
    prisma?: InputType[]    // Prisma generated input types
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]     // User model output types
    prisma: OutputType[]    // Prisma generated output types (Query, Mutation)
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}
```

#### InputType & SchemaArg (Arguments)
```
InputType = {
  name: string
  constraints: {
    maxNumFields: number | null
    minNumFields: number | null
    fields?: string[]
  }
  meta?: { source?: string; grouping?: string }
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}

SchemaArg = {
  name: string
  comment?: string
  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean
  requiresOtherFields?: string[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
}
```

#### OutputType & SchemaField (Output Structure)
```
OutputType = {
  name: string
  fields: SchemaField[]
}

SchemaField = {
  name: string
  isNullable?: boolean
  outputType: OutputTypeRef
  args: SchemaArg[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
  documentation?: string
}
```

#### Mappings (Model → Operations)
```
ModelMapping = {
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string
  findFirst?: string
  findFirstOrThrow?: string
  findMany?: string
  create?: string
  createMany?: string
  createManyAndReturn?: string
  update?: string
  updateMany?: string
  updateManyAndReturn?: string
  upsert?: string
  delete?: string
  deleteMany?: string
  aggregate?: string
  groupBy?: string
  count?: string
  findRaw?: string
  aggregateRaw?: string
}
```

### 2. GeneratorOptions (What's Passed to Generators)

**Location:** `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
GeneratorOptions = {
  generator: GeneratorConfig           // This generator's configuration
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]   // Other generators being run
  schemaPath: string                   // Path to schema.prisma file
  dmmf: DMMF.Document                  // Complete DMMF (the key input!)
  datasources: DataSource[]            // Database connection info
  datamodel: string                    // Raw Prisma schema as string
  version: string                      // Version hash
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}

GeneratorConfig = {
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null              // Output directory
  provider: EnvValue
  config: {[key: string]: string | string[]}
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}

DataSource = {
  name: string
  provider: 'mysql' | 'mongodb' | 'sqlite' | 'postgresql' | ...
  activeProvider: ConnectorType
  schemas: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}
```

### 3. Generator Handler Protocol

**Location:** `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`

Generators implement the `Handler` interface:

```typescript
interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

GeneratorManifest = {
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: {
    models?: string[]          // Models to exclude
    fields?: string[]          // Fields to exclude
  }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}
```

**Communication Protocol:**
- Uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout
- Generator receives `{ method: 'generate', params: GeneratorOptions }`
- Generator receives `{ method: 'getManifest', params: GeneratorConfig }`
- Generator responds on stderr with JSON-RPC responses
- Example generator: `packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/minimal-executable`

### 4. How client-generator-ts Processes DMMF

**Architecture:**
1. **Entry Point:** `generator.ts` implements `Generator` interface
2. **Receives:** `GeneratorOptions` with `dmmf: DMMF.Document`
3. **Processing:** `generateClient()` in `generateClient.ts`
4. **Core:** `TSClient` class coordinates everything
5. **DMMFHelper:** Wraps DMMF.Document and provides indexed lookup

**DMMFHelper (packages/client-generator-ts/src/dmmf.ts):**
```typescript
class DMMFHelper {
  document: DMMF.Document
  
  // Cached lookups:
  typeAndModelMap: Dictionary<DMMF.Model>
  mappingsMap: Dictionary<DMMF.ModelMapping>
  outputTypeMap: { prisma: {}, model: {} }
  rootFieldMap: Dictionary<DMMF.SchemaField>
  
  // Methods:
  isComposite(name: string): boolean
  resolveInputObjectType(ref: InputTypeRef): InputType | undefined
  resolveOutputObjectType(ref: OutputTypeRef): OutputType | undefined
}
```

**Model Processing Flow:**
1. `Model` class (TSClient/Model.ts) takes `DMMF.Model`
2. Creates TypeScript interfaces/types using ts-builders
3. For each action (find, create, update, etc.):
   - Gets schema field from root Query/Mutation via DMMFHelper
   - Builds Args types using `ArgsTypeBuilder`
   - Generates Select, Include, Omit helper types
4. Output types built from `DMMF.OutputType` fields

**File Generators:**
- `ModelFile.ts` - Creates individual model files
- `ModelsFile.ts` - Barrel export for all models
- `EnumsFile.ts` - All enums
- `CommonInputTypesFile.ts` - Shared input types
- `PrismaNamespaceFile.ts` - Main Prisma namespace
- etc.

### 5. TypeScript Code Generation with ts-builders

**Location:** `packages/ts-builders/src/`

**Core Builder Classes:**
- `TypeBuilder` (abstract) - Base for all type expressions
- `Writer` - Streams output with indentation tracking
- `Property` - Field in interface/object
- `Interface` - `interface { ... }`
- `Method` - Function in interface
- `TypeDeclaration` - `type X = ...`
- `NamedType` - Reference to named type
- `UnionType` - `A | B | C`
- `IntersectionType` - `A & B & C`
- `ArrayType` - `T[]`
- `ObjectType` - `{ key: Type }`
- `DocComment` - JSDoc comment

**Usage Pattern:**
```typescript
const property = ts.property('userId', ts.namedType('number'))
  .optional()
  .setDocComment(ts.docComment('User ID'))

const iface = ts.interfaceDeclaration('User')
  .add(property)
  .add(ts.property('name', ts.namedType('string')))

const typeDecl = ts.typeDeclaration('UserCreateInput', 
  ts.objectType().add(property))

const exported = ts.moduleExport(typeDecl)
const stringified = ts.stringify(exported)
```

**Building Model Output Types (Output.ts):**
```
Field (DMMF) → TypeBuilder (ts-builders) → String (stringify)

Example:
- Field: { name: 'id', type: 'Int', isRequired: true, isList: false }
- TypeBuilder: ts.namedType('number')
- Property: property('id', ts.namedType('number'))
- String: "id: number"
```

### 6. Test Patterns

**Generator Test:**
- Path: `packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/generatorHandler.test.ts`
- Pattern: Create GeneratorProcess, call `init()`, then `generate()` or `getManifest()`
- Uses stub DMMF with empty models/enums

**Client Generator Test:**
- Path: `packages/client-generator-ts/tests/generator.test.ts`
- Pattern: Uses actual schema file, passes through registry, asserts generated files

### 7. Key Takeaways

1. **DMMF is the Bridge**: It's a complete intermediate representation - every field, type, relation, and operation is encoded in DMMF before TypeScript generation

2. **Two-Layer Schema**: 
   - Datamodel layer (models, fields, relations)
   - Schema layer (GraphQL-like input/output types for API)

3. **Generator Protocol**:
   - JSON-RPC based stdin/stdout communication
   - Handlers receive full DMMF, metadata, and config
   - Can declare capabilities via GeneratorManifest

4. **Code Generation Pipeline**:
   - Parse DMMF → Create TypeScript AST (ts-builders) → Stringify to text
   - Generators have full TypeScript ecosystem available

5. **Helper Classes**:
   - `DMMFHelper` indexes DMMF for efficient lookups
   - `GenerateContext` threads configuration through generators
   - `Writer` handles multi-line formatted output with indentation

---

**Files to Reference:**
- Core DMMF types: `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`
- Generator protocol: `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`
- GeneratorOptions type: `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`
- ts-builders utilities: `/packages/ts-builders/src/`
- Client generator implementation: `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/`
- Working example: `/packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/minimal-executable`

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive exploration report. Let me create the summary:

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Prisma Generator Packages Structure

Based on thorough examination of the monorepo's Prisma generator packages, here's a detailed breakdown of how the generator ecosystem is structured:

### 1. **GENERATOR PROTOCOL LAYER** (`@prisma/generator`)
**Location**: `/packages/generator/`

**Purpose**: Defines the core types and interfaces for all generators.

**Key Files**:
- `src/index.ts` - Exports all generator types
- `src/configuration.ts` - Defines the GeneratorConfig, GeneratorOptions, and GeneratorManifest interfaces
- `src/generator.ts` - Defines the Generator interface that all generators must implement
- `src/typedSql.ts` - Defines SQL query output types

**Core Types**:
```typescript
interface GeneratorConfig {
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null
  isCustomOutput?: boolean
  provider: EnvValue
  config: Record<string, string | string[] | undefined>
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}

interface GeneratorOptions {
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
  schemaPath: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string
  version: string
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}

interface Generator {
  readonly name: string
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}

type GeneratorManifest = {
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: { models?: string[], fields?: string[] }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}
```

**Build Config**: Uses `unbundledConfig` - ships as unbundled ESM/CJS with external dependencies marked.

---

### 2. **GENERATOR HELPER / PROTOCOL IMPLEMENTATION** (`@prisma/generator-helper`)
**Location**: `/packages/generator-helper/`

**Purpose**: Provides the JSON-RPC communication layer and handler infrastructure for generators.

**Key Files**:
- `src/index.ts` - Main exports
- `src/generatorHandler.ts` - Core handler that implements the JSON-RPC protocol
- `src/GeneratorProcess.ts` - Spawns and communicates with child generator processes
- `src/json-rpc.ts` - JSON-RPC message type definitions

**Handler Interface**:
```typescript
interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
// Reads from stdin, listens for JSON-RPC messages
// Writes responses to stderr as JSON
```

**GeneratorProcess Class** (for spawning child generators):
- Spawns a child process (Node or shell command)
- Establishes bidirectional JSON-RPC communication via stdin/stderr
- Methods:
  - `init()` - Spawns the child process
  - `getManifest(config)` - Calls 'getManifest' RPC method
  - `generate(options)` - Calls 'generate' RPC method
  - `stop()` - Gracefully terminates with SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after 2s
- Handles errors, process lifecycle, and message routing

**JSON-RPC Protocol**:
```typescript
type Request = { jsonrpc: '2.0', method: string, params?: any, id: number }
type SuccessResponse = { jsonrpc: '2.0', result: any, id: number }
type ErrorResponse = { jsonrpc: '2.0', error: { code: number, message: string, data: any }, id: number }
```

**Build Config**: Uses ESM split to CJS, bundles dependencies, marks external: `@prisma/debug`, `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`.

---

### 3. **TYPESCRIPT CLIENT GENERATOR** (`@prisma/client-generator-ts`)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-ts/`

**Purpose**: Generates TypeScript Prisma Client code.

**Structure**:
- `src/generator.ts` - Main generator class implementing `Generator` interface
- `src/generateClient.ts` - Core generation logic
- `src/TSClient/` - 27 TypeScript generation modules
- `src/dmmf.ts` - DMMFHelper for DMMF manipulation
- `src/getDMMF.ts` - DMMF retrieval utilities
- `src/file-extensions.ts` - File extension handling for TS/MTS/CTS/JS
- `src/module-format.ts` - Module format detection (ESM/CJS)
- `src/runtime-targets.ts` - Runtime target handling (nodejs, deno, workerd, vercel-edge)

**Main Generator Class**:
```typescript
class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
  
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

**GenerateClientOptions Interface**:
```typescript
interface GenerateClientOptions {
  datamodel: string
  schemaPath: string
  runtimeBase: string
  outputDir: string
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  binaryPaths: BinaryPaths
  engineVersion: string
  clientVersion: string
  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
  target: RuntimeTargetInternal // 'nodejs' | 'deno' | 'workerd' | 'vercel-edge'
  generatedFileExtension: GeneratedFileExtension // 'ts' | 'mts' | 'cts'
  importFileExtension: ImportFileExtension // '.ts' | '.mjs' | '.cjs' | '.js'
  moduleFormat: ModuleFormat // 'esm' | 'cjs'
  tsNoCheckPreamble: boolean
  compilerBuild: 'fast' | 'small'
}
```

**Generation Flow**:
1. Parse generator config (output, runtime target, file extensions, module format)
2. Load TypeScript config if available
3. Call `generateClient()` which:
   - Builds client using `TSClient` class
   - Validates DMMF against denylists (reserved keywords)
   - Applies typed SQL generation if provided
   - Adds WASM file map
   - Adds "@ts-nocheck" preamble if configured
4. Write FileMap to disk with proper directory structure

**TSClient Class** (`src/TSClient/TSClient.ts`):
- Orchestrates generation of all client files
- Creates: client.ts, browser.ts, enums.ts, commonInputTypes.ts, models.ts, models/*.ts
- Internal files: prismaNamespace.ts, prismaNamespaceBrowser.ts, class.ts
- Uses GenerateContext for template data passing

**DMMFHelper** (`src/dmmf.ts`):
Wraps DMMF.Document with caching:
- `typeAndModelMap` - Combined models + composite types
- `outputTypeMap` - Output types by namespace (prisma/model)
- `inputTypesByName` - Input types lookup
- `mappingsMap` - Model operation mappings
- `rootFieldMap` - Root Query/Mutation fields

**Build Config**: Uses `adapterConfig` - bundled, ESM output with splitting.

---

### 4. **JAVASCRIPT CLIENT GENERATOR** (`@prisma/client-generator-js`)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-js/`

**Purpose**: Generates JavaScript Prisma Client code (used in older/legacy contexts).

**Key Differences from TS Generator**:
- `src/generator.ts` - `PrismaClientJsGenerator` class
- Has `shouldResolvePrismaClient` option (for resolving `@prisma/client` package location)
- Has `shouldInstallMissingPackages` option
- Uses `resolvePrismaClient()` to find package path
- Copies runtime files to output directory
- Handles `copyRuntime` and `copyRuntimeSourceMaps` options
- Otherwise largely mirrors TypeScript version structure

**Generator Class**:
```typescript
class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-js'
  
  constructor(options?: {
    shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
    shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
    runtimePath?: string
  })
  
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

**Build Config**: Same as TypeScript (`adapterConfig`).

---

### 5. **CLIENT GENERATOR REGISTRY** (`@prisma/client-generator-registry`)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-registry/`

**Purpose**: Central registry for generator instantiation.

**Key Files**:
- `src/registry.ts` - `GeneratorRegistry` class
- `src/default.ts` - Default registry with built-in generators

**GeneratorRegistry**:
```typescript
class GeneratorRegistry {
  add(generator: Generator): void
  addAliased(name: string, generator: Generator): void
  toInternal(): IGeneratorRegistry // Converts to internal format
}
```

**Default Registry** (`src/default.ts`):
```typescript
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())
const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator) // Alias
```

Registers generators by name:
- `prisma-client-js` → PrismaClientJsGenerator
- `prisma-client-ts` → PrismaClientTsGenerator
- `prisma-client` → PrismaClientTsGenerator (aliased)

---

### 6. **DIRECTORY STRUCTURES**

**generator-helper/src**:
```
src/
├── __tests__/
│   ├── generatorHandler.test.ts (JSON-RPC protocol tests)
│   ├── exiting-executable, failing-executable, etc. (test fixtures)
│   └── readline-utf8-split.test.ts
├── generatorHandler.ts (JSON-RPC handler)
├── GeneratorProcess.ts (child process spawner)
├── json-rpc.ts (message types)
└── index.ts (exports)
```

**client-generator-ts/src**:
```
src/
├── TSClient/
│   ├── file-generators/ (11 file generators)
│   ├── TSClient.ts (orchestrator)
│   ├── Model.ts, PrismaClient.ts, TypeMap.ts, etc.
│   └── utils/
├── typedSql/ (typed SQL query generation)
├── utils/ (helper utilities)
├── dmmf.ts (DMMF helper class)
├── generateClient.ts (main generation function)
├── generator.ts (Generator implementation)
├── getDMMF.ts (DMMF utilities)
├── file-extensions.ts (TS/MTS/CTS/JS handling)
├── module-format.ts (ESM/CJS detection)
├── runtime-targets.ts (runtime target parsing)
└── index.ts (exports)
```

**client-generator-js/src**:
```
src/
├── TSClient/ (similar structure to TS version)
├── typedSql/
├── utils/
├── dmmf.ts
├── generateClient.ts (more complex with runtime copying)
├── generator.ts (Generator implementation)
├── getDMMF.ts
├── resolvePrismaClient.ts (package resolution)
└── index.ts
```

---

### 7. **INVOCATION FLOW**

**How generators are used in the system**:

1. **Registration Phase**:
   - DefaultRegistry instantiates and registers all generators
   - Generators implement the Generator interface

2. **Configuration Phase** (Prisma schema parsing):
   - Schema defines generators with configuration
   - GeneratorConfig created from schema

3. **Invocation Phase** (prisma generate):
   - For in-process generators (client-generator-ts, client-generator-js):
     - Directly call `generator.getManifest()` and `generator.generate()`
   - For external generators:
     - Spawn via GeneratorProcess using JSON-RPC protocol
     - Send 'getManifest' and 'generate' RPC calls

4. **Generation Phase**:
   - Generator receives GeneratorOptions (DMMF, datasources, config, etc.)
   - Generates TypeScript/JavaScript files
   - Returns FileMap with generated code
   - Files written to configured output directory

---

### 8. **BUILD SYSTEM**

**Helper Build Scripts**:
- `generator/helpers/build.ts` → uses `unbundledConfig`
- `generator-helper/helpers/build.ts` → uses `esmSplitCodeToCjs`
- `client-generator-ts/helpers/build.ts` → uses `adapterConfig`
- `client-generator-js/helpers/build.ts` → uses `adapterConfig`

**Build Outputs**:
- Each package compiles to `dist/` with both ESM and CJS variants
- TypeScript source in `src/` compiles to both formats
- ESM imports used internally, CJS for compatibility

---

### 9. **KEY DEPENDENCIES & IMPORTS**

**generator-helper depends on**:
- `@prisma/generator` (types)
- `@prisma/dmmf` (type definitions)
- `@prisma/debug` (logging)

**client-generator-ts depends on**:
- `@prisma/generator` (base types)
- `@prisma/dmmf` (DMMF definitions)
- `@prisma/client-common` (shared utilities)
- `@prisma/ts-builders` (TypeScript generation helpers)
- `@prisma/param-graph-builder` (parameter graph construction)
- `@prisma/internals` (internal utilities)
- `fast-glob`, `ts-pattern`, `klona`, `indent-string`, etc.

**client-generator-js** has similar dependencies plus:
- `env-paths` (for cache/config paths)

---

This architecture enables:
1. **Composability**: Generators can depend on each other via `requiresGenerators`
2. **Extensibility**: Custom generators can implement the Generator interface
3. **Process Isolation**: External generators run in separate processes via JSON-RPC
4. **Type Safety**: Strong typing via GeneratorConfig and GeneratorOptions
5. **Multiple Output Formats**: Support for TypeScript, JavaScript, different module formats
6. **Runtime Flexibility**: Support for Node.js, Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Runtime

- `src/index.ts` - Main exports
- `src/generatorHandler.ts` - Core handler that implements the JSON-RPC protocol
- `src/GeneratorProcess.ts` - Spawns and communicates with child generator processes
- `src/json-rpc.ts` - JSON-RPC message type definitions

**Handler Interface**:
```typescript
interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
// Reads from stdin, listens for JSON-RPC messages
// Writes responses to stderr as JSON
```

**GeneratorProcess Class** (for spawning child generators):
- Spawns a child process (Node or shell command)
- Establishes bidirectional JSON-RPC communication via stdin/stderr
- Methods:
  - `init()` - Spawns the child process
  - `getManifest(config)` - Calls 'getManifest' RPC method
  - `generate(options)` - Calls 'generate' RPC method
  - `stop()` - Gracefully terminates with SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after 2s
- Handles errors, process lifecycle, and message routing

**JSON-RPC Protocol**:
```typescript
type Request = { jsonrpc: '2.0', method: string, params?: any, id: number }
type SuccessResponse = { jsonrpc: '2.0', result: any, id: number }
type ErrorResponse = { jsonrpc: '2.0', error: { code: number, message: string, data: any }, id: number }
```

**Build Config**: Uses ESM split to CJS, bundles dependencies, marks external: `@prisma/debug`, `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`.

---

### 3. **TYPESCRIPT CLIENT GENERATOR** (`@prisma/client-generator-ts`)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-ts/`

**Purpose**: Generates TypeScript Prisma Client code.

**Structure**:
- `src/generator.ts` - Main generator class implementing `Generator` interface
- `src/generateClient.ts` - Core generation logic
- `src/TSClient/` - 27 TypeScript generation modules
- `src/dmmf.ts` - DMMFHelper for DMMF manipulation
- `src/getDMMF.ts` - DMMF retrieval utilities
- `src/file-extensions.ts` - File extension handling for TS/MTS/CTS/JS
- `src/module-format.ts` - Module format detection (ESM/CJS)
- `src/runtime-targets.ts` - Runtime target handling (nodejs, deno, workerd, vercel-edge)

**Main Generator Class**:
```typescript
class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
  
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

**GenerateClientOptions Interface**:
```typescript
interface GenerateClientOptions {
  datamodel: string
  schemaPath: string
  runtimeBase: string
  outputDir: string
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  binaryPaths: BinaryPaths
  engineVersion: string
  clientVersion: string
  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
  target: RuntimeTargetInternal // 'nodejs' | 'deno' | 'workerd' | 'vercel-edge'
  generatedFileExtension: GeneratedFileExtension // 'ts' | 'mts' | 'cts'
  importFileExtension: ImportFileExtension // '.ts' | '.mjs' | '.cjs' | '.js'
  moduleFormat: ModuleFormat // 'esm' | 'cjs'
  tsNoCheckPreamble: boolean
  compilerBuild: 'fast' | 'small'
}
```

**Generation Flow**:
1. Parse generator config (output, runtime target, file extensions, module format)
2. Load TypeScript config if available
3. Call `generateClient()` which:
   - Builds client using `TSClient` class
   - Validates DMMF against denylists (reserved keywords)
   - Applies typed SQL generation if provided
   - Adds WASM file map
   - Adds "@ts-nocheck" preamble if configured
4. Write FileMap to disk with proper directory structure

**TSClient Class** (`src/TSClient/TSClient.ts`):
- Orchestrates generation of all client files
- Creates: client.ts, browser.ts, enums.ts, commonInputTypes.ts, models.ts, models/*.ts
- Internal files: prismaNamespace.ts, prismaNamespaceBrowser.ts, class.ts
- Uses GenerateContext for template data passing

**DMMFHelper** (`src/dmmf.ts`):
Wraps DMMF.Document with caching:
- `typeAndModelMap` - Combined models + composite types
- `outputTypeMap` - Output types by namespace (prisma/model)
- `inputTypesByName` - Input types lookup
- `mappingsMap` - Model operation mappings
- `rootFieldMap` - Root Query/Mutation fields

**Build Config**: Uses `adapterConfig` - bundled, ESM output with splitting.

---

### 4. **JAVASCRIPT CLIENT GENERATOR** (`@prisma/client-generator-js`)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-js/`

**Purpose**: Generates JavaScript Prisma Client code (used in older/legacy contexts).

**Key Differences from TS Generator**:
- `src/generator.ts` - `PrismaClientJsGenerator` class
- Has `shouldResolvePrismaClient` option (for resolving `@prisma/client` package location)
- Has `shouldInstallMissingPackages` option
- Uses `resolvePrismaClient()` to find package path
- Copies runtime files to output directory
- Handles `copyRuntime` and `copyRuntimeSourceMaps` options
- Otherwise largely mirrors TypeScript version structure

**Generator Class**:
```typescript
class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-js'
  
  constructor(options?: {
    shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
    shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
    runtimePath?: string
  })
  
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

**Build Config**: Same as TypeScript (`adapterConfig`).

---

### 5. **CLIENT GENERATOR REGISTRY** (`@prisma/client-generator-registry`)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-registry/`

**Purpose**: Central registry for generator instantiation.

**Key Files**:
- `src/registry.ts` - `GeneratorRegistry` class
- `src/default.ts` - Default registry with built-in generators

**GeneratorRegistry**:
```typescript
class GeneratorRegistry {
  add(generator: Generator): void
  addAliased(name: string, generator: Generator): void
  toInternal(): IGeneratorRegistry // Converts to internal format
}
```

**Default Registry** (`src/default.ts`):
```typescript
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())
const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator) // Alias
```

Registers generators by name:
- `prisma-client-js` → PrismaClientJsGenerator
- `prisma-client-ts` → PrismaClientTsGenerator
- `prisma-client` → PrismaClientTsGenerator (aliased)

---

### 6. **DIRECTORY STRUCTURES**

**generator-helper/src**:
```
src/
├── __tests__/
│   ├── generatorHandler.test.ts (JSON-RPC protocol tests)
│   ├── exiting-executable, failing-executable, etc. (test fixtures)
│   └── readline-utf8-split.test.ts
├── generatorHandler.ts (JSON-RPC handler)
├── GeneratorProcess.ts (child process spawner)
├── json-rpc.ts (message types)
└── index.ts (exports)
```

**client-generator-ts/src**:
```
src/
├── TSClient/
│   ├── file-generators/ (11 file generators)
│   ├── TSClient.ts (orchestrator)
│   ├── Model.ts, PrismaClient.ts, TypeMap.ts, etc.
│   └── utils/
├── typedSql/ (typed SQL query generation)
├── utils/ (helper utilities)
├── dmmf.ts (DMMF helper class)
├── generateClient.ts (main generation function)
├── generator.ts (Generator implementation)
├── getDMMF.ts (DMMF utilities)
├── file-extensions.ts (TS/MTS/CTS/JS handling)
├── module-format.ts (ESM/CJS detection)
├── runtime-targets.ts (runtime target parsing)
└── index.ts (exports)
```

**client-generator-js/src**:
```
src/
├── TSClient/ (similar structure to TS version)
├── typedSql/
├── utils/
├── dmmf.ts
├── generateClient.ts (more complex with runtime copying)
├── generator.ts (Generator implementation)
├── getDMMF.ts
├── resolvePrismaClient.ts (package resolution)
└── index.ts
```

---

### 7. **INVOCATION FLOW**

**How generators are used in the system**:

1. **Registration Phase**:
   - DefaultRegistry instantiates and registers all generators
   - Generators implement the Generator interface

2. **Configuration Phase** (Prisma schema parsing):
   - Schema defines generators with configuration
   - GeneratorConfig created from schema

3. **Invocation Phase** (prisma generate):
   - For in-process generators (client-generator-ts, client-generator-js):
     - Directly call `generator.getManifest()` and `generator.generate()`
   - For external generators:
     - Spawn via GeneratorProcess using JSON-RPC protocol
     - Send 'getManifest' and 'generate' RPC calls

4. **Generation Phase**:
   - Generator receives GeneratorOptions (DMMF, datasources, config, etc.)
   - Generates TypeScript/JavaScript files
   - Returns FileMap with generated code
   - Files written to configured output directory

---

### 8. **BUILD SYSTEM**

**Helper Build Scripts**:
- `generator/helpers/build.ts` → uses `unbundledConfig`
- `generator-helper/helpers/build.ts` → uses `esmSplitCodeToCjs`
- `client-generator-ts/helpers/build.ts` → uses `adapterConfig`
- `client-generator-js/helpers/build.ts` → uses `adapterConfig`

**Build Outputs**:
- Each package compiles to `dist/` with both ESM and CJS variants
- TypeScript source in `src/` compiles to both formats
- ESM imports used internally, CJS for compatibility

---

### 9. **KEY DEPENDENCIES & IMPORTS**

**generator-helper depends on**:
- `@prisma/generator` (types)
- `@prisma/dmmf` (type definitions)
- `@prisma/debug` (logging)

**client-generator-ts depends on**:
- `@prisma/generator` (base types)
- `@prisma/dmmf` (DMMF definitions)
- `@prisma/client-common` (shared utilities)
- `@prisma/ts-builders` (TypeScript generation helpers)
- `@prisma/param-graph-builder` (parameter graph construction)
- `@prisma/internals` (internal utilities)
- `fast-glob`, `ts-pattern`, `klona`, `indent-string`, etc.

**client-generator-js** has similar dependencies plus:
- `env-paths` (for cache/config paths)

---

This architecture enables:
1. **Composability**: Generators can depend on each other via `requiresGenerators`
2. **Extensibility**: Custom generators can implement the Generator interface
3. **Process Isolation**: External generators run in separate processes via JSON-RPC
4. **Type Safety**: Strong typing via GeneratorConfig and GeneratorOptions
5. **Multiple Output Formats**: Support for TypeScript, JavaScript, different module formats
6. **Runtime Flexibility**: Support for Node.js, Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Runtime

3	import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
4	import { getTsconfig } from 'get-tsconfig'
5	import { bold, dim, green } from 'kleur/colors'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { inferImportFileExtension, parseGeneratedFileExtension, parseImportFileExtension } from './file-extensions'
9	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
10	import { inferModuleFormat, parseModuleFormatFromUnknown } from './module-format'
11	import { parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown, RuntimeTargetInternal } from './runtime-targets'
12	
13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}
29	
30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}
104
